senses.definition: a small, furry, long-eared mammal that hops and eats plants
examples: A rabbit is hopping in the garden.
She kept a pet rabbit in a hutch.

senses.definition: informal term for a brave or foolish person in a playful or teasing way
examples: Don't be a rabbit—face the challenge!
He acts like a rabbit when confronted with danger.

senses.definition: plural form referring to berries eaten by rabbits or a plant bearing such berries; may be used regionally or humorously
examples: The rabbits nibble on rabbitberries in the field.
Gardeners noted rabbitberries among the wild fruit.

senses.definition: a hypothetical or obscure plant berry linked to rabbits; may appear in folk names or local usage
examples: The trail mentioned a plant called rabbitberry.
Some local legends talk about rabbitberry bushes.

senses.definition: a North American shrub with yellow flowers, used to describe a plant in dry areas
examples: The trail passes through rabbitbrush stands.
Birds shelter among the rabbitbrush in late summer.

senses.definition: also written as 'rabbit brush'; a common name for certain shrubs in the genus Chrysothamnus or Ericameria
examples: The landscape was dotted with rabbitbrush.
We identified several rabbitbrush colonies.

senses.definition: plural of rabbitbrush; multiple shrubs of the rabbitbrush type
examples: We saw several rabbitbrushes along the plains.
The field contained rabbitbrushes in bloom.

senses.definition: past tense of rabbit; to run away quickly or to hunt rabbits (colloquial)
examples: The fox rabbited into the bushes.
They rabbited the field to avoid the dogs.

senses.definition: someone who rabbites; a person who hunts or pursues rabbits; rare term
examples: The village rabiter tracks wild rabbits.
A skilled rabiter keeps a close eye on burrows.

senses.definition: plural of rabbiter; people who rabbit (hunt rabbits) or manage rabbits
examples: The rabbiters gathered for the hunt.
Rabbiters often use trained dogs.

senses.definition: a type of plant fruit or a brand name; also a common name for certain plant diseases or berries associated with rabbits in some regions
examples: Rabbiteye blueberries are common in the southern US.
The gardener planted rabbiteye shrubs near the path.

senses.definition: a fish of the family Siganidae, known for a rabbit-like face and small size
examples: We spotted a rabbitfish at the reef.
Rabbitfish swim near the coral to feed.

senses.definition: plural of rabbitfish
examples: We observed several rabbitfishes during snorkeling.
Different rabbitfishes inhabit the lagoon.

senses.definition: kind, gentle, and brave; typical of a rabbit's perceived innocence
examples: She showed rabbithearted kindness to all the pets.
A rabbithearted approach can be calming.

senses.definition: present participle of rabbit; to search for rabbits or to run away; also informal talk or chitchat in a lively way
examples: The hunter was rabbiting through the field.
They spent the afternoon rabbiting about town.

senses.definition: plural of rabbiting; instances of rabbiting or chitchat
examples: Her rabbitings lasted all afternoon.
We had a few rabbitings about the old days.

senses.definition: resembles a rabbit in appearance or behavior
examples: The creature has rabbitlike ears and a twitching nose.
Its eyes are rabbitlike in size and shape.

senses.definition: a mouth that resembles a rabbit's or a name for an animal feature; may appear in regional usage or descriptions
examples: The sculpture features a rabbitmouth grin.
In some dialects, rabbitmouth describes a wide mouth.

senses.definition: a rare or regional term; possibly a child's term or a misspelling; no standard meaning in broad English
examples: Rabbito appears in a local dialect dictionary.
The term rabbito is not widely used in modern English.

senses.definition: an old or regional term for a person who hunts rabbits or a member of a rugby team nickname used in some places
examples: The rabbitohs won the match.
The old hunter was called a rabbitoh.

senses.definition: plural of rabbitoh; a professional rugby league team name in Australia (South Sydney Rabbitohs)
examples: The Rabbitohs celebrated their victory.
Fans wore red and green for the Rabbitohs.

senses.definition: plural of rabbit-o; a playful or fictional term; may refer to rabbit-like creatures or be a branding/name in fiction
examples: In the story, several rabbitos hid in the brush.
The toy line features cartoon rabbitos.

senses.definition: designed to prevent rabbits from entering or damaging an area; as in a rabbit-proof fence or barrier
examples: A rabbitproof fence was built to protect the crops.
They installed rabbitproof netting around the garden.

senses.definition: plural of rabbitry; places where rabbits are kept or bred; a breeding facility
examples: The farm operates several rabbitries.
Rabbit breeders visited the rabbitries to inspect stock.

senses.definition: a plant or root used in folklore; may refer to a specific root used by rabbits or a medicinal plant alias
examples: The herbalist spoke about rabbitroot as a remedy.
Some gardeners cultivate rabbitroot for soil health.

senses.definition: the place where rabbits are kept or bred; a rabbit farm or collection
examples: The farmer expanded the rabbitry to meet demand.
She visited the rabbitry to choose a pet rabbit.

senses.definition: the plural of rabbit; more than one rabbit
examples: There are several rabbits in the garden.
Two rabbits hopped across the yard.
